questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a Pennsylvania salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania requires 75 hours of pre-license education from a PREC-approved school. The coursework must be completed within 5 years prior to taking the licensing exam.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed their 75-hour pre-license course 6 years ago. Can they sit for the PA salesperson exam?
distractors: Yes, with 14 hours of CE added
Yes, if they retake one course
questions.explanation: Pre-license education must be completed within 5 years prior to the exam. At 6 years, the coursework has expired and the candidate must retake the required education.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Pennsylvania salesperson license?
questions.explanation: The minimum age for a PA salesperson license is 18. The broker license requires a minimum age of 21 plus 3 years of experience as a licensed salesperson.

questions.question_text: A Pennsylvania salesperson applicant must submit a criminal background check from which agency?
questions.explanation: Applicants must provide a Pennsylvania State Police criminal background report dated within 90 days of the application. This is separate from any federal background check.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson has not yet found an employing broker. The salesperson's license status is:
questions.explanation: A PA salesperson license is inactive without an employing broker. The licensee cannot perform any licensed activities until they are associated with a licensed broker.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a PA licensee complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania requires 14 hours of CE every 2-year renewal cycle. This must include 3 hours of agency, 2 hours of fair housing, and 9 hours of electives.

questions.question_text: Of the 14 required CE hours per cycle, how many must cover fair housing topics?
questions.explanation: Each CE cycle requires 3 hours of agency, 2 hours of fair housing, and 9 hours of electives for a total of 14 hours. The fair housing component ensures licensees stay current on anti-discrimination laws.

questions.question_text: Pennsylvania real estate licenses must be renewed by what date?
questions.explanation: All PA real estate licenses expire on May 31 of even-numbered years (e.g., 2026, 2028). Licensees must complete 14 hours of CE and renew by this deadline.

questions.question_text: A licensee completing their first renewal after initial licensure must complete a special:
questions.explanation: At their first renewal, new licensees must complete a mandatory 14-hour post-license course. This is in addition to the regular CE requirements for subsequent renewal cycles.

questions.question_text: Which license type allows a broker-qualified individual to practice under another broker's supervision?
questions.explanation: An associate broker holds a broker-level license but works under another broker rather than operating independently. This allows individuals with broker qualifications to gain experience or preference working for another firm.

questions.question_text: A licensee's PA license has been inactive for 4 years. To reactivate without re-examination, the licensee must:
distractors: Submit a new criminal background check only
questions.explanation: Within 5 years of inactivity, a licensee can reactivate by completing 14 hours of CE. After 5 years, the licensee must retake the licensing examination.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's license has been expired for 6 years. What must they do to become licensed again?
distractors: Pay a late renewal fee only
questions.explanation: The 5-year window for reactivation without re-examination has passed. After 5 years of inactivity or expiration, the licensee must retake the licensing exam to become licensed again.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A to join Broker B. The transfer is processed through which system?
questions.explanation: Changes of employment are processed through PALS (Pennsylvania Licensing System). Both the former broker (to release) and new broker (to accept) must process the transfer through this online portal.

questions.question_text: An agent licensed in Maryland wants a Pennsylvania license. Under reciprocity, the agent must:
questions.correct_answer: Take only the PA state portion of the exam
distractors: Take both national and state portions
Complete 75 hours of PA pre-license first
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania has reciprocity with several states including Maryland. Applicants licensed in a reciprocal state within the past 5 years need only pass the PA state exam portion, not the national portion.

questions.question_text: When a salesperson changes brokers, the salesperson's license status during any gap between employers is:
questions.explanation: A salesperson's license is inactive whenever they are not associated with an employing broker. During a gap between employers, the licensee cannot perform any licensed real estate activities.
